_comment: Affinity scores per (category, consultant). 10 = perfect match, 1 = poor match. Lookup order: (1) consultant NOT in known_consultants -> default_score (5). (2) category present in 'categories' -> categories[category][consultant] (with cell-missing fallback to default_score=5). (3) category absent -> general_score (8). NOTE the asymmetry: a known consultant in an unknown category gets 8, but in a known category with no cell gets 5. Rationale: a missing cell in a known category is a configuration gap (caught by doctor.sh) and should de-rank the consultant; an unknown category is just unmapped routing and treats all known consultants equally. To override at runtime: AFFINITY_FILE=/path/to/custom.json.
_comment_capabilities: v1.1: per-consultant capability axes (1-10, higher=better) in 'capabilities', plus 'category_axis' mapping each question category to the quality axis it stresses. intelligence + taste are QUALITY axes: they weight votes (voting.sh, ENABLE_CAPABILITY_WEIGHTING) and rank the panel (routing.sh, ENABLE_CAPABILITY_ROUTING). cost is an EFFICIENCY axis for budget-aware composition only and is NEVER a vote weight (tie-break order: intelligence > taste > cost). Missing consultant/axis -> capability_default. Both features are OPT-IN and additive: with the flags off, behavior is identical to v1.0. These scores are SUBJECTIVE point-in-time seeds — re-derive them for your own panel (see the model-routing methodology): Claude (claude-opus-4-8) and Codex (gpt-5.5) axes are grounded on the author's model-routing table; the other consultants' axes are heuristic seeds pending measurement. Unmapped category_axis -> intelligence.
